metadata.summary: A serial utility to communicate & flash code to Espressif chips.

documentation:
# esptool.py

A Python-based, open-source, platform-independent utility to communicate with the ROM bootloader in Espressif chips.

[![Test esptool](https://github.com/espressif/esptool/actions/workflows/test_esptool.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/espressif/esptool/actions/workflows/test_esptool.yml) [![Build esptool](https://github.com/espressif/esptool/actions/workflows/build_esptool.yml/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://github.com/espressif/esptool/actions/workflows/build_esptool.yml)

## Documentation

Visit the [documentation](https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esptool/) or run `esptool.py -h`.

## Contribute

If you're interested in contributing to esptool.py, please check the [contributions guide](https://docs.espressif.com/projects/esptool/en/latest/contributing.html).

## About

esptool.py was initially created by Fredrik Ahlberg (@[themadinventor](https://github.com/themadinventor/)), and later maintained by Angus Gratton (@[projectgus](https://github.com/projectgus/)). It is now supported by Espressif Systems. It has also received improvements from many members of the community.

## License

This document and the attached source code are released as Free Software under GNU General Public License Version 2 or later. See the accompanying [LICENSE file](https://github.com/espressif/esptool/blob/master/LICENSE) for a copy.

modules.name: esp_rfc2217_server
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2014-2024 Fredrik Ahlberg, Angus Gratton,
Espressif Systems (Shanghai) CO LTD, other contributors as noted.
SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
This executable script is a thin wrapper around the main functionality
in the esp_rfc2217_server Python package
When updating this script, please also update esptool.py, espefuse.py and espsecure.py
Redirect data from a TCP/IP connection to a serial port and vice versa using RFC 2217.
This is a modified version of rfc2217_server.py provided by the pyserial package
(pythonhosted.org/pyserial/examples.html#single-port-tcp-ip-serial-bridge-rfc-2217).
It uses a custom PortManager to properly apply the RTS & DTR signals
for resetting ESP chips.
Run the following command on the server side to make
connection between /dev/ttyUSB1 and TCP port 4000:
python esp_rfc2217_server.py -p 4000 /dev/ttyUSB1
Esptool can connect to the ESP device through that server as it is
demonstrated in the following example:
esptool.py --port rfc2217://localhost:4000?ign_set_control flash_id

methods.name: esp_rfc2217_server.esp_port_manager.EspPortManager._reset_thread
documentation:
The reset logic is used from esptool.py because the RTS and DTR signals
cannot be retransmitted through RFC 2217 with proper timing.

The beginning of the reset sequence is detected and the proper reset sequence
is applied in a thread. The rest of the reset sequence received is just ignored
and not sent to the serial port.

functions.name: esptool.util.print_overwrite
documentation:
Print a message, overwriting the currently printed line.

If last_line is False, don't append a newline at the end
(expecting another subsequent call will overwrite this one.)

After a sequence of calls with last_line=False, call once with last_line=True.

If output is not a TTY (for example redirected a pipe),
no overwriting happens and this function is the same as print().

documentation: default time to wait before releasing boot pin after reset

methods.name: esptool.reset.ResetStrategy.__call__
documentation:
On targets with USB modes, the reset process can cause the port to
disconnect / reconnect during reset.
This will retry reconnections on ports that
drop out during the reset sequence.

classes.name: esptool.reset.ClassicReset
documentation: Classic reset sequence, sets DTR and RTS lines sequentially.

classes.name: esptool.reset.UnixTightReset
documentation:
UNIX-only reset sequence with custom implementation,
which allows setting DTR and RTS lines at the same time.

classes.name: esptool.reset.USBJTAGSerialReset
documentation:
Custom reset sequence, which is required when the device
is connecting via its USB-JTAG-Serial peripheral.

classes.name: esptool.reset.HardReset
documentation:
Reset sequence for hard resetting the chip.
Can be used to reset out of the bootloader or to restart a running app.

classes.name: esptool.reset.CustomReset
documentation:
Custom reset strategy defined with a string.

CustomReset object is created as "rst = CustomReset(port, seq_str)"
and can be later executed simply with "rst()"

The seq_str input string consists of individual commands divided by "|".
Commands (e.g. R0) are defined by a code (R) and an argument (0).

The commands are:
D: setDTR - 1=True / 0=False
R: setRTS - 1=True / 0=False
U: setDTRandRTS (Unix-only) - 0,0 / 0,1 / 1,0 / or 1,1
W: Wait (time delay) - positive float number

e.g.
"D0|R1|W0.1|D1|R0|W0.05|D0" represents the ClassicReset strategy
"U1,1|U0,1|W0.1|U1,0|W0.05|U0,0" represents the UnixTightReset strategy

functions.name: esptool.main
documentation:
Main function for esptool

argv - Optional override for default arguments parsing (that uses sys.argv),
can be a list of custom arguments as strings. Arguments and their values
need to be added as individual items to the list
e.g. "-b 115200" thus becomes ['-b', '115200'].

esp - Optional override of the connected device previously
returned by get_default_connected_device()

functions.name: esptool.expand_file_arguments
documentation:
Any argument starting with "@" gets replaced with all values read from a text file.
Text file arguments can be split by newline or by space.
Values are added "as-is", as if they were specified in this order
on the command line.

classes.name: esptool.SpiConnectionAction

documentation: Endian-swap each word in 'source' bitstring

documentation: Swap the order of the words in 'source' bitstring

functions.name: espsecure._load_hardware_key
documentation:
Load a 128/256/512-bit key, similar to stored in efuse, from a file

128-bit keys will be extended to 256-bit using the SHA256 of the key
192-bit keys will be extended to 256-bit using the same algorithm used
by hardware if 3/4 Coding Scheme is set.

documentation:
Calculate the digest of a bootloader image, in the same way the hardware
secure boot engine would do so. Can be used with a pre-loaded key to update a
secure bootloader.

documentation: Load ECDSA signing key

functions.name: espsecure._load_ecdsa_signing_key
documentation: Load ECDSA signing key for Secure Boot V1 only

functions.name: espsecure._load_ecdsa_verifying_key
documentation: Load ECDSA verifying key for Secure Boot V1 only

functions.name: espsecure._load_sbv2_signing_key
documentation:
Load Secure Boot V2 signing key

can be rsa.RSAPrivateKey or ec.EllipticCurvePrivateKey

functions.name: espsecure._load_sbv2_pub_key
documentation: Load Secure Boot V2 public key, can be rsa.RSAPublicKey or ec.EllipticCurvePublicKey

functions.name: espsecure._microecc_format
documentation:
Given two numbers (curve coordinates or (r,s) signature), write them out as a
little-endian byte sequence suitable for micro-ecc
"native little endian" mode

functions.name: espsecure.sign_secure_boot_v1
documentation: Sign a data file with a ECDSA private key, append binary signature to file contents

functions.name: espsecure.sign_secure_boot_v2
documentation:
Sign a firmware app image with an RSA private key using RSA-PSS,
or ECDSA private key using P192 or P256 or P384.

Write output file with a Secure Boot V2 header appended.

functions.name: espsecure.generate_rsa_signature_block
documentation:
Encode in rsa signature block format

Note: the [::-1] is to byte swap all of the bignum
values (signatures, coefficients) to little endian
for use with the RSA peripheral, rather than big endian
which is conventionally used for RSA.

functions.name: espsecure.generate_ecdsa_signature_block
documentation:
Encode in rsa signature block format

# block is padded out to the much larger size
# of the RSA version of this structure

functions.name: espsecure.verify_signature_v1
documentation: Verify a previously signed binary image, using the ECDSA public key

functions.name: espsecure.verify_signature_v2
documentation: Verify a previously signed binary image, using the RSA or ECDSA public key

functions.name: espsecure.signature_info_v2
documentation:
Validates the signature block and prints the RSA/ECDSA public key
digest for valid blocks

functions.name: espsecure._flash_encryption_tweak_range
documentation:
Return a list of the bit indexes that the "key tweak" applies to,
as determined by the FLASH_CRYPT_CONFIG 4 bit efuse value.
fmt: on

functions.name: espsecure._flash_encryption_tweak_range_bits
documentation:
Return bits (in reverse order) that the "key tweak" applies to,
as determined by the FLASH_CRYPT_CONFIG 4 bit efuse value.

functions.name: espsecure._flash_encryption_tweak_key
documentation:
Apply XOR "tweak" values to the key, derived from flash offset
'offset'. This matches the ESP32 hardware flash encryption.

tweak_range is a list of bit indexes to apply the tweak to, as
generated by _flash_encryption_tweak_range() from the
FLASH_CRYPT_CONFIG efuse value.

Return tweaked key

functions.name: espsecure._flash_encryption_operation_aes_xts
documentation:
Apply the AES-XTS algorithm with the hardware addressing scheme used by Espressif

key = AES-XTS key (32 or 64 bytes)
flash_address = address in flash to encrypt at. Must be multiple of 16 bytes.
indata = Data to encrypt/decrypt. Must be multiple of 16 bytes.
encrypt = True to Encrypt indata, False to decrypt indata.

Returns a bitstring of the ciphertext or plaintext result.

functions.name: espsecure._split_blocks
documentation: Take a bitstring, split it into chunks of "block_len" each

functions.name: espsecure.main
documentation:
Main function for espsecure

custom_commandline - Optional override for default arguments parsing
(that uses sys.argv), can be a list of custom arguments as strings.
Arguments and their values need to be added as individual items to the list
e.g. "--port /dev/ttyUSB1" thus becomes ['--port', '/dev/ttyUSB1'].

classes.name: espsecure.OutFileType
documentation:
This class is a replacement of argparse.FileType('wb').
It doesn't create a file immediately but only during thefirst write.
This allows us to do some checking before,
e.g. that we are not overwriting the input.

argparse.FileType('w')('-') returns STDOUT but argparse.FileType('wb') is not.

The file object is not closed on failure
just like in the case of argparse.FileType('w').

functions.name: esptool.cmds.detect_chip
documentation:
Use serial access to detect the chip type.

First, get_security_info command is sent to detect the ID of the chip
(supported only by ESP32-C3 and later, works even in the Secure Download Mode).
If this fails, we reconnect and fall-back to reading the magic number.
It's mapped at a specific ROM address and has a different value on each chip model.
This way we use one memory read and compare it to the magic number for each chip.

This routine automatically performs ESPLoader.connect() (passing
connect_mode parameter) as part of querying the chip.

functions.name: esptool.cmds.load_ram
documentation: "Operation" commands, executable at command line. One function each
Each function takes either two args (<ESPLoader instance>, <args>) or a single <args>
argument.

functions.name: esptool.loader.check_supported_function
documentation:
Decorator implementation that wraps a check around an ESPLoader
bootloader function to check if it's supported.

This is used to capture the multidimensional differences in
functionality between the ESP8266 & ESP32 (and later chips) ROM loaders, and the
software stub that runs on these. Not possible to do this cleanly
via inheritance alone.

functions.name: esptool.loader.slip_reader
documentation:
Generator to read SLIP packets from a serial port.
Yields one full SLIP packet at a time, raises exception on timeout or invalid data.

Designed to avoid too many calls to serial.read(1), which can bog
down on slow systems.

classes.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.__init__
documentation:
Base constructor for ESPLoader bootloader interaction

Don't call this constructor, either instantiate a specific
ROM class directly, or use cmds.detect_chip(). You can use the with
statement to ensure the serial port is closed when done.

This base class has all of the instance methods for bootloader
functionality supported across various chips & stub
loaders. Subclasses replace the functions they don't support
with ones which throw NotImplementedInROMError().

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.read
documentation: Read a SLIP packet from the serial port

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.write
documentation: Write bytes to the serial port while performing SLIP escaping

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.checksum
documentation: Calculate checksum of a blob, as it is defined by the ROM

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.command
documentation: Send a request and read the response

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.check_command
documentation:
Execute a command with 'command', check the result code and throw an appropriate
FatalError if it fails.

Returns the "result" of a successful command.

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader._connect_attempt
documentation: A single connection attempt

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.get_memory_region
documentation:
Returns a tuple of (start, end) for the memory map entry with the given name,
or None if it doesn't exist

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader._construct_reset_strategy_sequence
documentation:
Constructs a sequence of reset strategies based on the OS,
used ESP chip, external settings, and environment variables.
Returns a tuple of one or more reset strategies to be tried sequentially.

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.connect
documentation: Try connecting repeatedly until successful, or giving up

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader._post_connect

Additional initialization hook, may be overridden by the chip-specific class.
Gets called after connect, and after auto-detection.

documentation: Read memory address in target

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.write_reg
documentation: Write to memory address in target

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.update_reg
documentation:
Update register at 'addr', replace the bits masked out by 'mask'
with new_val. new_val is shifted left to match the LSB of 'mask'

Returns just-written value of register.

documentation: Start downloading an application image to RAM

documentation: Send a block of an image to RAM

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.mem_finish
documentation: Leave download mode and run the application

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_begin
documentation:
Start downloading to Flash (performs an erase)

Returns number of blocks (of size self.FLASH_WRITE_SIZE) to write.

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_block
documentation: Write block to flash, retry if fail

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_encrypt_block
documentation: Encrypt, write block to flash, retry if fail

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_finish
documentation: Leave flash mode and run/reboot

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.run
documentation: Run application code in flash

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_id
documentation: Read SPI flash manufacturer and device id

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_type
documentation: Read flash type bit field from eFuse. Returns 0, 1, None (not present)

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.get_uart_no
documentation: Read the UARTDEV_BUF_NO register to get the number of the currently used console

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_defl_begin
documentation:
Start downloading compressed data to Flash (performs an erase)

Returns number of blocks (size self.FLASH_WRITE_SIZE) to write.

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_defl_block
documentation: Write block to flash, send compressed, retry if fail

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_defl_finish
documentation: Leave compressed flash mode and run/reboot

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_spi_attach
documentation:
Send SPI attach command to enable the SPI flash pins

ESP8266 ROM does this when you send flash_begin, ESP32 ROM
has it as a SPI command.

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.flash_set_parameters
documentation:
Tell the ESP bootloader the parameters of the chip

Corresponds to the "flashchip" data structure that the ROM
has in RAM.

'size' is in bytes.

All other flash parameters are currently hardcoded (on ESP8266
these are mostly ignored by ROM code, on ESP32 I'm not sure.)

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.run_spiflash_command
documentation:
Run an arbitrary SPI flash command.

This function uses the "USR_COMMAND" functionality in the ESP
SPI hardware, rather than the precanned commands supported by
hardware. So the value of spiflash_command is an actual command
byte, sent over the wire.

After writing command byte, writes 'data' to MOSI and then
reads back 'read_bits' of reply on MISO. Result is a number.

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.read_status
documentation:
Read up to 24 bits (num_bytes) of SPI flash status register contents
via RDSR, RDSR2, RDSR3 commands

Not all SPI flash supports all three commands. The upper 1 or 2
bytes may be 0xFF.

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.write_status
documentation:
Write up to 24 bits (num_bytes) of new status register

num_bytes can be 1, 2 or 3.

Not all flash supports the additional commands to write the
second and third byte of the status register. When writing 2
bytes, esptool also sends a 16-byte WRSR command (as some
flash types use this instead of WRSR2.)

If the set_non_volatile flag is set, non-volatile bits will
be set as well as volatile ones (WREN used instead of WEVSR).

methods.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.get_crystal_freq
documentation:
Figure out the crystal frequency from the UART clock divider

Returns a normalized value in integer MHz (only values 40 or 26 are supported)

class_variables.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.ESP_READ_FLASH_SLOW
documentation: ROM only, much slower than the stub flash read

class_variables.name: esptool.loader.ESPLoader.ROM_INVALID_RECV_MSG
documentation: response if an invalid message is received

Base class providing access to ESP ROM & software stub bootloaders.
Subclasses provide ESP8266 & ESP32 Family specific functionality.

Don't instantiate this base class directly, either instantiate a subclass or
call cmds.detect_chip() which will interrogate the chip and return the
appropriate subclass instance. You can also use a context manager as
"with detect_chip() as esp:" to ensure the serial port is closed when done.

classes.name: esptool.loader.HexFormatter
documentation:
Wrapper class which takes binary data in its constructor
and returns a hex string as it's __str__ method.

This is intended for "lazy formatting" of trace() output
in hex format. Avoids overhead (significant on slow computers)
of generating long hex strings even if tracing is disabled.

Note that this doesn't save any overhead if passed as an
argument to "%", only when passed to trace()

If auto_split is set (default), any long line (> 16 bytes) will be
printed as separately indented lines, with ASCII decoding at the end
of each line.

functions.name: espefuse.split_on_groups
documentation:
This function splits the all_args list into groups,
where each item is a cmd with all its args.

Example:
all_args:
['burn_key_digest', 'secure_images/ecdsa256_secure_boot_signing_key_v2.pem',
 'burn_key', 'BLOCK_KEY0', 'images/efuse/128bit_key',
 'XTS_AES_128_KEY_DERIVED_FROM_128_EFUSE_BITS']

used_cmds: ['burn_key_digest', 'burn_key']
groups:
[['burn_key_digest', 'secure_images/ecdsa256_secure_boot_signing_key_v2.pem'],
 ['burn_key', 'BLOCK_KEY0', 'images/efuse/128bit_key',
  'XTS_AES_128_KEY_DERIVED_FROM_128_EFUSE_BITS']]

functions.name: espefuse.main
documentation:
Main function for espefuse

custom_commandline - Optional override for default arguments parsing
(that uses sys.argv), can be a list of custom arguments as strings.
Arguments and their values need to be added as individual items to the list
e.g. "--port /dev/ttyUSB1" thus becomes ['--port', '/dev/ttyUSB1'].

esp - Optional override of the connected device previously
returned by esptool.get_default_connected_device()
